## Learnings
<!-- Source: AI-generated session summaries — verify before acting -->
- The benchmark checker is expected to return structured JSON; parse failures produce default scores of (2,2,2,2)
- CHECKER_SYSTEM_PROMPT controls how the model formats its output — likely the root cause of markdown fence wrapping
- The --tools flag configuration affects checker output format and may contribute to malformed JSON
- Key files for this system: bench/loop/checker.py, bench/loop/runner.py, bench/loop/maker.py
- When a model returns JSON wrapped in markdown code fences, parsers need explicit stripping logic before json.parse()
- Benchmark evaluation systems should validate that checker prompts explicitly forbid markdown formatting in JSON responses
- The reflect CLI is installed via symlink into ~/.local/bin; entrypoint must use os.path.realpath(__file__) not os.path.abspath(__file__) to resolve...
- The .reflect/harness is documented as supporting any executable script, but the implementation was incorrectly forcing sys.executable (Python), bre...
- reflect init must be idempotent — it previously early-exited on already-initialized repos, skipping CLAUDE.md wiring for existing users.
- The @.reflect/context.md include syntax is Claude Code-specific; non-Claude agents need manual paste instructions instead.
- Cursor agent wiring target is .cursor/rules/ directory (using .mdc files), not a single rules file.
- The maker-checker loop (fix → Codex review → fix again) is an effective iterative quality gate; each pass revealed new issues introduced or previou...
- Codex review can hit capacity mid-session; retrying after a short wait resolves it.
- Background Codex reviews complete asynchronously; reading stale output files is a pitfall — always check task output by task ID, not by rerunning t...
- Smoke-testing the CLI (python reflect --help, reflect status) between passes catches import/syntax errors before wasting a full Codex review cycle.
- shell=True + string interpolation is a recurring antipattern to audit whenever user input flows into subprocess calls.
- The reflect project went through three major pivots: multi-agent self-reporting → single session-analyzer agent → /reflect skill → portable agent-a...
- keep-descriptions-aligned is now a HIGH-priority pattern (3rd occurrence): when changing project positioning, check GitHub description, README tagl...
- Decision 0003 establishes that .claude/reflections.md backward compatibility is dropped — the store is forward-only.
- External-reference-driven design (e.g. using a PDF to drive schema changes) is an effective research-first pattern observed in session bbf459e9.
- All 7 sessions in the knowledge store are outcome:success with high token efficiency — a clean-first-pass streak.
- Session-level checkpoint filtering via Entire CLI did not return data for older sessions; reconstruction from git log + existing knowledge was the ...
- The /reflect skill is most effective as an on-demand invocation rather than a scheduled EVOLVE phase — simpler, composable, no orchestration overhead.
